---
title: "FW 536 — Day 4 morning lab (v2 · avian / songbird) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 4 morning lab — Maximum likelihood problem set

**Problem set version:** **v2 — avian / songbird theme**.
(The companion version is [v3 — marine & stock assessment](problem_set_v3.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

> **Note on topic scope.** The morning lecture covers **maximum likelihood**. The Bayesian content comes in the afternoon. The problems below use only what was taught this morning (likelihood ideas, MLE by hand, numerical optimization, mixture models). The remaining problems in `problem_set_v2.html` are reserved for the **afternoon Bayesian lab**.

This template matches [`problem_set_v2.html`](problem_set_v2.html). Write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

---

# Problem 1 — Yellow Warbler nest survival

> You monitored 24 Yellow Warbler nests through the egg-laying and incubation periods. Each nest either successfully hatched at least one chick ($y_i = 1$) or failed ($y_i = 0$). Out of 24 nests, 17 succeeded; the remaining 7 failed (depredation or abandonment).

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 2 — Black-capped Chickadee count MLE

> During point-count surveys at $n = 30$ stations you observed counts $y_i$ of Black-capped Chickadees, totaling $\sum y_i = 84$ birds. Treat counts as iid Poisson($\lambda$).

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 3 — Hummingbird arrival times — two-component Normal mixture

> You record the arrival time of $n=200$ Rufous Hummingbirds at a feeder over a single morning, measured in minutes after sunrise. Field intuition suggests two cohorts — early risers and a later wave — modeled as a 2-component Normal mixture:

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every morning problem from `problem_set_v2.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
